headline: 14-Year-Old Mountaineer Plants Pakistan Flag at Peak
meta_description: At just 14, Malaika Khilji from Balochistan has made history by summiting Khosar Gang and raising Pakistan's national flag at the peak.
summary_short: Malaika Khilji, a 14-year-old climber from Balochistan, has etched her name in Pakistan's mountaineering history by conquering Khosar Gang.
body:
At just 14 years old, Malaika Khilji has etched her name in Pakistan's mountaineering history by conquering Khosar Gang and raising the national flag at the summit.

The young climber from Balochistan has shattered expectations about what teenage climbers can achieve, breaking barriers in a sport where youth participation remains limited. Her ascent of Khosar Gang represents a milestone not just for her personally, but for mountaineering in Pakistan—a country with some of the world's most challenging peaks.

Khilji's accomplishment signals a shifting landscape in Pakistani athletics and adventure sports, where younger generations are pushing into territories previously dominated by older, more established climbers. By standing atop Khosar Gang with her country's flag, she has inspired others to consider what's possible at any age.
quick_facts: She planted Pakistan's national flag at the peak
